spans.content: Deconvolving detector distortions - critical step to compare
·Theoretical predictions often at the level of moments.
New approach to directly unfold distribution moments
as a function of other observables without discretizing data
Demonstrate the performance of this approach using jet
in experimental data - necessary for accurately comparing data
between experiments and with theoretical predictions.
· Typically, entire spectra unfolded -moments computed afterward.
· This binning procedure introduces discretization artifacts.
Unfold without binning-generic solution to unfolding entire
spectra-may compromise precision for small set of moments.
Dedicated machine learning-based unfolding method to directly
GAN learns reweighting function inspired by the Boltzman
equation- parameters identified with observable moments
Noniterative in contrast to methods like Omnifold (Andreassen
g ( x ) = e ^ { \lambda _ { 1 } x + \cdots + \lambda _ { n } x ^ { n } }
· Moment unfolding maximises BCE loss holding moments constant

L [ g, d ] = - \frac {1}{N} \sum_ {x _ {\mathrm {D}}} \left[ \log (d (x _ {\mathrm {D}})) + \sum_ {(x _ {\mathrm {G}}, x _ {\mathrm {S}})} g (x _ {\mathrm {G}}) \log (1 - d (x _ {\mathrm {S}})) \right]
· All neural networks implemented using the KERAs high-level API
parametrized with three hidden layers,50 nodes
w = \frac {1}{p _ {T , j e t}} \sum_ {i \in \text {j e t}} p _ {T, i} \Delta R _ {i}
·Example focusses on jet width. Method can be applied to other
jet observables like charge, mass, etc.
•First two moments of the reweighted generation match truth well
This is because higher moments are relevant and are not the same
Proposed Moment Unfolding as a novel, flexible,unbinned, and
Showed promising results when applied to both Gaussian datasets
as well as detector data from the LHC
Future work-whether this method could be used to unfold
Deconvolving Detector Effects for Distribution Moments
Krish Desai, Benjamin Nachman, Jesse Thaler
